extraction.one_line_summary: PORT Richie won the 23.5km ITT by 12 seconds over Martin and 37 over Froome, taking second overall behind surprise yellow jersey DUMOULIN Thomas.
tactical_patterns: Port beat Froome by 37 seconds in 23.5km ITT despite Froome's recent dominance, suggesting form hierarchy shift.
Dumoulin held yellow by finishing top-10 in ITT as climbing specialist, signaling improved time trial capacity.
Valverde limited losses to 1 second/km against specialist Port, revealing unusually strong TT form for stage racer.

rider_signals.PORT Richie: positive: Beat Martin by 12s and Froome by 37s in 23.5km, confirming Tour de Romandy TT dominance translates to Dauphine form peak
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost 37 seconds to Port in 23.5km despite characteristic effort, already 10s down at mid-race checkpoint
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Lost 2 minutes to Martin as second-place GC rider, exposing major ITT weakness before mountain stages
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Thomas: positive: Finished top-10 in ITT to retain yellow despite climbing specialist profile, defying expectations from Tour de France stage-2 collapse
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Third place losing only 1s/km to Port reveals exceptional TT form for typically inconsistent time trialist
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: neutral: World champion set 28:19 benchmark but beaten by Port, suggesting peaked form timing off
